title: Technical documentation paragraph
description: Full technical doc passage — terms + structure + flow
text: The authentication module uses a stateless JWT design. Each request must include a valid Bearer token in the Authorization header. Tokens expire after fifteen minutes; clients should call the refresh endpoint before that window closes. All errors return a structured JSON object with status, code, and message fields.

tips.text: Technical writing turns up everywhere now, not just in engineering. The clearer it reads, the more it is worth.
Treat JWT, JSON, Bearer as whole words. Spell them out letter by letter and the sentence loses its stride.

tips.target: The authentication module uses a stateless JWT design. Each request must include a valid Bearer token in the Authorization header. Tokens expire after fifteen

tips.text: Even dry instructions have a cadence. Find it and the hardest passage starts to read like plain prose.

tips.target: minutes; clients should call the refresh endpoint before that window closes. All errors return a structured JSON object with status, code, and message fields.

character_tips.anna: Documentation at speed is a skill that travels across every corner of tech. Clear instructions are never wasted work. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Most people who build things write far more than they build. Type the documents cleanly and the work gets used. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Don't rush the jargon. The terms are the load-bearing words; get them right and the rest carries itself. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Clear instructions, plainly set down - like good teaching notes. Keep them tidy and someone will thank you for it. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
